title: Compliance — Animal Research (IACUC)
content:
The Animal Research Facility (ARF) at Morgan State University is located in the Martin D. Jenkins Behavioral and Social Sciences Center. Built in 2017, the 2,725-square-foot vivarium and research facility was constructed, staffed, and equipped to the standards of the American Association for Accreditation of Laboratory Animal Care (AAALAC). It supports laboratory animal studies in an innovative, ethical, and humane manner, with housing for rats, mice, and small aquatic species in dedicated aquarium space. All work performed at the facility must be approved by the Morgan State University Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) and adhere to occupational health and safety guidelines. The ARF maintains continuous compliance with federal Animal Welfare Act requirements for laboratory animal care and use. The Facility Manager, Caretaker, and Veterinarian are American Association for Laboratory Animal Science (AALAS) certified. The IACUC convenes monthly on the second Thursday from 1:00 to 3:00 PM; protocol submissions are due the third Thursday of the prior month. Investigators access the facility and consultations through MSU's Microsoft Bookings link (https://outlook.office365.com/owa/calendar/AnimalResearchFacilityMartinJenkinsHall@MorganStateUniversity365.onmicrosoft.com/bookings/) or via the iLab Solutions service-center page (registration with MSU credentials takes up to 24 hours; account at my.ilabsolutions.com/service_center/show_external/6195/research_infrastructure_core). Training and consultation services include: (a) Health and safety training workshops offered semi-annually and by appointment; (b) Basic rodent handling, animal care, and health-monitoring training by appointment; (c) Consultations for research design and IACUC protocol writing by appointment; (d) Individualized training for specialized surgical, behavioral, or other technical procedures by appointment. The IACUC publishes a comprehensive set of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) covering training requirements, rodent import/export, anesthesia and analgesia, hazards, animal purchasing and receiving, breeding, waste management, cage labeling and record-keeping, cage washing, controlled substances access, carcass disposal, cleaning, emergency and disaster planning, environmental enrichment, euthanasia (including alternatives to CO2), transport, identification, non-pharmaceutical-grade drugs, mouse breeding colony management, feed and bedding ordering, PPE, pharmaceutical drugs, facility access, rodent quarantine, occupational health program, sentinel procedures, density and overcrowding policy, survival surgery, cabinet cage washer use, veterinary clinical checks, water testing, weekend/holiday care, microisolator-cage aseptic technique, post-approval monitoring and protocol renewal, parasite treatment, satellite areas, laboratory safety, vendor health-report review, and IACUC governance (protocol review, semiannual review, member appointment, and IACUC responsibilities). Animal welfare concerns can be reported anonymously through a Google Form at forms.gle/ivF2WuiqG566uWZGA. Key staff: the IACUC Director is Dr. Ingrid Tulloch (Ingrid.Tulloch@morgan.edu); the Manager is Rysheem McGirt (Rysheem.Mcgirt@morgan.edu); the Clinical Veterinarian is Dr. Matthew Terzi (M.Terzi@morgan.edu); the Consulting Veterinarian is Dr. Julie Watson. Investigators seeking training resources should email Dr. Tulloch directly.

--- Housing Capacity (verified 2026-05-14) ---
Total capacity 1,836 animals across the ARF, broken down as: Rats — 720 in individually ventilated cages (IVC), 2 rooms, 2 animals per cage, 30 cages per rack, 12 racks. Mice — 960 in IVC, 2 rooms, 4 animals per cage, 80 cages per rack, 3 racks; plus 60 in individual biometric housing with 24-hour activity monitoring (1 room, 1 animal per cage, 60 cages per rack, 1 rack). Aquaria — 96 fish, 1 room, 4 animals per tank, 12 tanks per rack, 2 racks.

--- Available Equipment (verified 2026-05-14) ---
Surgery: Two small-animal surgery tables (one backdraft, one downdraft), digitized stereotaxic instruments, gas and chemical anesthesia system, dry-ice machine, Kent Scientific vital-signs monitoring, autoclave. Histology: Biosafety Level II cabinet, chemical fume hood for perfusions, HistoCore Arcadia tissue processor/embedder, Leica RM2255 fully automated rotary microtome, hot plates, scales, pH meter, sonicator. Behavior Testing: Three rooms with Med Associates operant-conditioning chambers (fear conditioning, drug self-administration), acoustic chambers, light-dark boxes, modular mazes, activity chamber, Rotarod for mice/rats with video recording; manual open-field, Elevated Plus, and T-Maze for mice, rats, and small aquatic species. Molecular Laboratory: Hacker Bright cryostat, Keyence BZ-XB fluorescence microscope with live-cell imaging and automated cell counting, plate washer, Countess cell counter, NanoDrop Lite, gel electrophoresis, refrigerated centrifuge, digital temperature shaking incubator, Wes Simple Western blot protein analysis apparatus, Molecular Devices Emax-Plus microplate reader, plus standard small lab items.

--- IACUC Forms (verified 2026-05-14) ---
35 forms are published on the IACUC Forms page including: IACUC Protocol Form (with instructions), Protocol Amendment Request Form, Annual Review, Change PI Form, Post-Approval Monitoring Form, Animal Order Request Form, Animal Care Program Checklist, Animal Receiving Log, Animal Room Daily Monitoring Sheet, Animal Incident Form, Acceptable Enrichment Types, Rodent Breeding Cage Card / Form / Log, Dead Animal Card and Dead Animal Report, Controlled Drug Logs, Controlled Substances Vendors, Feeding and Bedding Receiving Log, Lab Colored Card Key, White Sticker with Letter Key, Autoclave Use Checklist, Cage Wash Log, Cage Washer Temperature Tape Log, NEU-ION Maintenance Log Sheet, Vivarium Orientation Checksheet, Facility Orientation and Training Checklist, Emergency Declaration and Point of Contact Call List, Environmental Concern Form, Medical Clearance and Proof of Immunization Form, Proof of Medical Clearance/Immunization/Occupational Health Risk Monitoring, Occupational Health Risk Assessment and Enrollment, OSHA Respirator Form, Post Operative Observation, HR0001/HR02 Accident Investigation.

--- SOPs (verified 2026-05-14) ---
50 SOPs are currently published (SOP 37 is not in the published list; numbering jumps from 36 to 38). Each SOP is a downloadable PDF hosted under /Documents/ADMINISTRATION/OFFICES/ora/Animal%20Research/SOPs/.
